pred_reason: The reentrancy vulnerability in release() directly corresponds to SWC-107 as it violates CEI pattern. The outdated Solidity version 0.4.18 relates to SWC-101 due to missing built-in overflow protections and security features. Evidence: release() makes external token transfer before updating isReleased state, enabling reentrancy attacks; Solidity 0.4.18 lacks automatic overflow checks and modern security protections; No reentrancy guards or built-in safety mechanisms present in the codebase

findings.Description: The release() function violates checks-effects-interactions pattern by making external token transfer call before updating isReleased state variable. This appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Malicious token contract could reenter and drain all tokens from the contract before state is updated
findings.Location: release() function, external call before isReleased = true

findings.Issue: Incorrect event emission order in transferOwnership()
findings.Description: OwnershipTransferred event is emitted before the owner state variable is updated. This appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: External systems monitoring events might receive incorrect state information, potential reentrancy risk
findings.Location: transferOwnership() function, event emission before state update

findings.Description: The lock() function can be called arbitrarily by owner, allowing control over when the 52-week period begins. This appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Owner can delay or prevent token release indefinitely by not calling lock() or calling it at arbitrary time

findings.Issue: External token call risk without error handling
findings.Description: token_reward.balanceOf(this) call lacks error handling and could revert, causing denial of service. This appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Contract could become unusable if token contract reverts on balanceOf calls, locking funds permanently
findings.Location: tokenBalance() function calling external token without error handling

findings.Description: Contract uses Solidity 0.4.18 which lacks modern security features and built-in protections. This appears in the smart contract code only.
findings.Impact: Missing built-in overflow checks, reentrancy guards, and other security features available in newer versions
